verdict_reasoning: EREG (epiregulin) is a single-pass transmembrane ligand of the EGF receptor family. It is synthesized as a membrane-anchored TM precursor with an N-terminal signal peptide, a large extracellular EGF-like ligand domain, a transmembrane domain, and a short cytoplasmic tail. The TM precursor resides stably on the cell surface and undergoes regulated ectodomain shedding by ADAM sheddases (particularly ADAM10/ADAM17), releasing the soluble EGF domain into solution. However, the membrane-anchored precursor form is the canonical biological entity: it supports juxtacrine signaling to neighboring cells and is the target of surface-directed therapies. Cell-surface biotinylation and flow-cytometry studies confirm stable cell-surface residence of the TM form. The protein is not transiently anchored and the TM stage is not dependent on unstable co-trafficking or cell-state induction — it is the default biogenesis pathway. This is the canonical single-pass TM receptor / ligand architecture.
